items.title: Study finds depression impacts all five senses
items.summary: SYDNEY: A new study examining the impact of depression on all five senses has found how depression is more than just a cognitive-emotional disorder, paving the way for more targeted treatments, reported Xinhua. Researchers from the University of New England (UNE) in Australia reported that people with depression showed significantly greater "sensory hyposensitivity" across all five senses compared with non-depressed individuals, according to a UNE statement released on Thursday. Sensory withdrawal is the body's way of coping with the pain, loss, stress, and conflict that can drive depression, Sharpley suggested.

items.title: Kota Damansara data centre plan sparks concern, Ramanan submits objection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Concerns have been raised over a proposed data centre in the residential area of Kota Damansara, with Sungai Buloh MP Datuk Seri R. Ramanan highlighting the lack of consultation with residents and their elected representative. The human resources minister said he had submitted an objection letter to the Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) mayor over the project. "Following this, my service centre also reviewed the proposed development to obtain a more comprehensive picture.

items.title: Negri Sembilan election: Bersatu to face Pas, Wawasan in eight seats
items.summary: Bersatu will also go up against Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's Parti Wawasan Negara in Klawang, Sikamat, Mambau and Gemas. At an event to unveil the party's candidates for the Negri Sembilan election, Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin reaffirmed that all candidates would contest under the Bersatu logo. Among the prominent candidates are Bersatu information chief Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz in Sikamat and Supreme Council member R. Sri Sanjeevan in Jeram Padang.

items.title: Azalina: RCI on 'corporate mafia' subject to probe outcome
items.summary: mafia' activities that could undermine the integrity of, and public confidence in, public institutions with grave concern, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. She said the ongoing investigations should be allowed to proceed independently, transparently and comprehensively, in accordance with the law and established procedures. She said the establishment of an RCI in Malaysia is governed by the Commissions of Enquiry Act 1950.

items.title: Malaysia banks seen primed for re-rating as regional rally matures
items.summary: JPMorgan said Malaysian banks offer attractive valuations underpinned by resilient fundamentals. The US investment bank said Malaysian lenders remain on a steady footing, supported by stable net interest margins (NIM), loan growth, cost efficiency, asset quality and capital management. Singapore banks have benefited from stronger loan growth, a favourable interest rate outlook and healthy fee income, although investors may become more sensitive to rising costs, non-performing loan (NPL) formation and credit costs.

items.title: Days of hope end in heartbreak as missing 10-year-old Sabah girl found dead, mother recalls unanswered prayers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — The mother of a 10-year-old Sabah girl found dead after days of desperate searching says she never stopped praying for her daughter’s safe return. “I never stopped praying. Nurjilya Abdullah disappeared on July 13.

items.title: International university intake does not affect local student admissions, says ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The admission of international students to public universities does not affect local students' chances of securing places, the Higher Education Ministry said. The ministry said the majority of international students were enrolled in postgraduate programmes, while undergraduate programmes at public universities continued to give priority to local students. Overall, enrolment across public and private higher education institutions stood at 1,264,541 students, with international students making up 12.6 per cent, or 159,138 students.

items.title: Natural river mixing likely behind Sungai Perak's bluish-green hue
items.summary: IPOH: The unusual bluish-green colouration seen in Sungai Perak near Kampung Bukit Amarikan, Gerik, is likely linked to a natural mixing process involving water from different rivers. "In the Hulu Perak case, a similar phenomenon may have occurred when water from Sungai Rui, which is normally murky, met Sungai Perak. "When JMG collected water samples from Sungai Perak, which appeared greenish previously, the water collected in the bottle was colourless and very clear," he said.

items.title: Coroner’s Court hears Zara Qairina distressed yet conscious, psychiatrist says she sought psychological safety
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 17 — The Coroner’s Court here heard that the late Zara Qairina Mahathir still had a conscious mind although she was distressed during her final encounter with a witness. Consultant psychiatrist Dr Wong Haw Huo told Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan that Zara was under the conscious mind as she was able to recognise the witness, who testified: “Wajahnya kelihatan merah seperti baru menangis” (Zara’s face looked red as if she had been crying). Under examination-in-chief by conducting officer deputy public prosecutor Mohd Fairuz Johari, the witness further testified that the first keywords mentioned by Zara were: “Saya mahu pindah sekolah” (I want to change schools).

items.title: Kulim water surge claims three lives, 4-year-old boy rescued
items.summary: KULIM: An outing involving four people ended in tragedy when three of them drowned following a sudden water surge in Karangan here today. Kulim Hi-Tech Fire and Rescue Station chief Mohammad Haiqal Izwan Nasir said the department received an emergency call at 2.27pm before rescuers were deployed to the scene. Meanwhile, Kulim police chief Superintendent Syamsul Sinring confirmed that all three victims had died in the incident.

items.title: Muhyiddin trial: Witness says Nepturis issued RM1mil cheque to Bersatu
items.summary: Nepturis managing director Mohd Rizman Akum Khan, 43, said businessman Lian Tan Chuan, also known as TC Lian, had instructed him to prepare the Maybank Islamic Bhd cheque dated Feb 16, 2022, payable to the political party. During earlier proceedings, Nepturis director and senior procurement executive Aliza Abd Malek, 47, testified that she and Mohd Rizman took over the company from TC Lian in 2018. Aliza had also testified that TC Lian held a 30 per cent interest in Nepturis because the company's capital came from capital injections by his company, Metrasys Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Subcontractor claims trial to firearms trafficking, ammunition charges
items.summary: KANGAR: A 54-year-old subcontractor pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to dealing in three firearms and possessing various imitation firearms and ammunition without lawful authority. Under the second charge, Yeong was accused of possessing an Airsoft rifle (4.5mm) and an Airsoft pistol (4.5mm) without lawful authority at the same location, time, and date. Under the third charge, framed under Section 36(1) of the same act, he was accused of possessing seven imitation Airsoft pistols (4.5mm) at the same location, time, and date.

items.title: Sabah Education Department says Papar student stable, urges calm after school fall incident
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 17 — The Sabah Education Department (JPN) today said preliminary investigations have confirmed the fall of a student in a Papar secondary school here but found no evidence of criminal elements or bullying in the incident. In a statement, the department said the student was taken to the hospital for examination and treatment. The department withheld the name of the school although it has been reported that it was the same school where a 13 year old student — Zara Qairina Mahathir — fell to her death almost exactly a year ago.

items.title: Bandar Utama school stabbing trial to hear evidence from three child witnesses under special safeguards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Three child witnesses are expected to give evidence under special court safeguards when the High Court next month hears the murder trial of a 15-year-old boy accused of killing his 16-year-old schoolmate, New Straits Times reported. Defence lawyer Kitson Foong said the prosecution plans to call 11 witnesses, including three children aged between 13 and 17 whose testimony will be governed by the Evidence of Child Witness Act 2007. “The prosecution will update the court on whether these child witnesses will require special arrangements to give evidence, including testifying from a separate courtroom via live television link if they are not comfortable giving evidence face-to-face in the witness box,” he said after a pre-trial case management before High Court judge Adlin Abdul Majid today.

items.title: Najib to undergo heart procedure next week [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak will undergo a heart procedure at Serdang Hospital next week. The former prime minister's special officer, Ahmad Lutfi Azhar, confirmed the matter when contacted today. Najib, 72, is currently serving a six-year prison sentence after being convicted in the SRC International Sdn Bhd case.

items.title: Three child witnesses among 11 in Bandar Utama school fatal stabbing trial
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A total of 11 prosecution witnesses are expected to testify in the trial of a boy charged with murdering his 16-year-old schoolmate in Bandar Utama, Petaling Jaya. The 15-year-old boy's defence lawyer, Kitson Foong, said three of the witnesses are children aged between 13 and 17, whose testimony will be governed by the Evidence of Child Witness Act 2007. He said this after a pre-trial case management before High Court judge Adlin Abdul Majid today.

items.title: Premier says Sarawak’s transformation driven by people’s ideas, government only implements policies
items.summary: MUKAH, July 17 — Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg today attributed the state’s rapid development to its people, saying unity, political stability and their readiness to embrace change have laid the foundation for Sarawak’s transformation. He said that while the government formulated policies and implemented development plans, the ideas and support came from the people. Abang Johari said Sarawak’s development agenda remains focused on balanced economic growth, infrastructure expansion and human capital development under the Post COVID-19 Development Strategy 2030 (PCDS 2030).

items.title: Former PM Najib to undergo heart procedure at Serdang Hospital next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak will undergo a heart procedure at Serdang Hospital next week, according to media reports today, as his family appealed for privacy during his treatment. Counsel Farhan Shafee confirmed to Free Malaysia Today (FMT) that Najib would undergo the procedure but said he could not reveal its timing or location. Najib’s special officer, Ahmad Lutfi Azhar, also told the newspaper that the former prime minister was in stable condition.

items.title: Puspakom to offer next-day inspections under new RM150 priority booking scheme from July 19
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Private vehicle owners will soon be able to pay for next-day Puspakom inspection appointments under a new premium booking service that the company says is designed to cater to urgent cases without reducing the availability of regular slots. According to Puspakom, the service is intended for private vehicle owners with time-sensitive inspection needs, including those completing ownership transfers or meeting financing requirements. Puspakom chief executive officer Mahmood Razak Bahman said the new service reflected the company’s commitment to offering customers more flexibility while preserving fair access to inspection appointments.

items.title: AmBank flags depleting oil reserves as biggest global threat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The depletion of global strategic petroleum reserves (SPR) could emerge as the biggest risk to the global economy in the second half of 2026, according to AmBank Group chief economist Firdaos Rosli. Although the global economy has remained resilient despite higher oil prices and the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, Firdaos warned that the energy buffer which has helped contain supply disruptions is steadily shrinking. However, those reserves have fallen sharply.

items.title: Malaysia records 5.8pc Q2 GDP growth, PM expresses gratitude after Friday prayers in Nilai
items.summary: NILAI, July 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed gratitude for Malaysia’s economic growth estimated at 5.8 per cent in the second quarter of 2026 after recording 5.4 per cent growth in the first quarter of this year. DOSM said in its statement that Malaysia’s inflation rate increased at a slower rate of 1.9 per cent in June 2026 compared to 2.0 per cent in the previous month. DOSM said the slower increase was mainly due to the transport sector, which recorded an increase of 2.8 per cent compared to 3.8 per cent in May 2026.

items.title: 'I never stopped praying': Mother's grief after missing girl found murdered [WATCH]
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: "I never stopped praying. Every day, I mentioned my daughter Nurjilya's name and asked Allah to show me where she was. In the end, Allah answered my prayers... my daughter's body was found."

items.title: China to raise domestic retail petrol, diesel prices from July 18
items.summary: BEIJING: China will raise domestic retail prices of petrol and diesel from Saturday, the state planner said on Friday, as the escalation of the US-Iran conflict lifted global oil prices. Caps for domestic retail petrol and diesel prices will go up by 300 yuan (US$44.28) per metric ton and 290 yuan per metric ton, respectively, the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) said, citing upward swings in global crude oil prices as reasons for adjustments. The NDRC reviews and adjusts official price ceilings for retail petrol and diesel every 10 working days.

items.title: PM grateful Malaysia's economy grows 5.8pct in second quarter of 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: NILAI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed gratitude for Malaysia's economic growth estimated at 5.8 per cent in the second quarter of 2026 after recording 5.4 per cent growth in the first quarter of this year. Overall, Malaysia's economy grew 5.6 per cent in the first half of this year compared to 4.5 per cent in the same period in 2025. DOSM said in its statement that Malaysia's inflation rate increased at a slower rate of 1.9 per cent in June 2026 compared to 2.0 per cent in the previous month.

items.title: ES Sunlogy surges on Kerjaya Prospek stake, trading hits eight-month high
items.summary: The stock, which opened one sen or 3.45 per cent higher at 30 sen, climbed as much as 15.52 per cent or 4.5 sen to 33.5 sen, a level not seen since Oct 31, 2025. Since then, the stock has traded between 20.5 sen and 45 sen. At 32.5 sen, the company had a market capitalisation of RM250.25 million. The stock has gained 6.56 per cent year to date, rising two sen from 30.5 sen at the start of the year.

items.title: Malaysian Bar president says regulator not a busybody as court allows intervention in MACC appeal
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 17 — The Court of Appeal today allowed the Malaysian Bar to intervene in a lawyer’s appeal against a High Court decision dismissing his application for leave to commence a judicial review over notices issued to him by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). However, the Bar’s counsel, Anand Raj, who is also the President of the Malaysian Bar, submitted that the Bar should be allowed to intervene as the outcome of the Court of Appeal would affect the profession. Lawyer Amanda Sonia Mathew, representing Mahajoth, supported the Malaysian Bar’s application.

items.title: Thailand, Cambodia maintain talks on border dispute, says Philippines
items.summary: MANILA: Thailand and Cambodia's agreement to sustain talks and pursue a solution to their existing border conflict during the May leaders' summit in Cebu continues to hold, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) said on Thursday, Philippine News Agency (PNA) reported. At a trilateral meeting led by President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr on May 7, Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet and Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul agreed to prevent actions that could escalate their dispute. "Whatever the decisions that the leaders had during the May summit, it's holding and Asean is very supportive," DFA spokesman for Asean Affairs Dax Imperial said in a press conference in Pasay City.

items.title: Puspakom rolls out fast-track inspection booking
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Puspakom Sdn Bhd has launched GiCheck Early Access Reservation (GEAR), allowing customers to secure faster inspection appointments at selected centres. The new service, which will be available from July 19, offers an additional appointment option when normal inspection slots are fully booked or when inspections are required within a short timeframe. The weekday-only service allows private vehicle owners, buyers and sellers completing ownership transfer to have a next-day appointment after booking.

items.title: China to raise domestic retail petrol, diesel prices from tomorrow
items.summary: BEIJING: China will raise domestic retail prices of petrol and diesel from Saturday, the state planner said on Friday, as the escalation of the US-Iran conflict lifted global oil prices. Caps for domestic retail petrol and diesel prices will go up by 300 yuan (RM181) per metric ton and 290 yuan per metric ton, respectively, the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) said, citing upward swings in global crude oil prices as reasons for adjustments. The NDRC reviews and adjusts official price ceilings for retail petrol and diesel every 10 working days.

items.title: Economy minister warns against complacency despite Malaysia’s resilient 5.8pc Q2 GDP growth
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir noted that the figure was higher than the 5.4 per cent recorded in the first quarter of this year, demonstrating that the country’s economic activity continued to expand despite geopolitical pressures. “We certainly welcome the 5.8 per cent growth on top of the 5.4 per cent recorded in the first quarter. When we look at economic growth, it is measured against the previous growth base.

items.title: More than 500 feared dead, including Rohingya, after boats sink off Myanmar: UN
items.summary: RAKHINE STATE: Two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off Myanmar's coast in recent days, UN agencies said on Thursday, as refugees from the war-torn country make perilous maritime journeys in search of safety and better lives. The two vessels left Myanmar's Rakhine State in late June carrying mostly ethnic minority Rohingya passengers, reportedly including some from refugee camps in Bangladesh, the International Organization for Migration and the UN's refugee agency said in a joint statement, citing preliminary information. Nearly 900 Rohingya refugees died or went missing in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal last year, making it the world's deadliest maritime route for refugees and migrants, according to the UN.

items.title: Food rider claims trial to reckless riding charge over fatal crash
items.summary: Muhammad Ridzuan Armis, 21, was charged with riding a Yamaha motorcycle recklessly, resulting in the death of Muhammad Nazri Shahadan, 47, on Jalan Dewan Bahasa, while travelling from Jalan Hang Tuah towards Jalan Istana, at about 2.30am on July 16. Prosecuting officer Inspector Shazwan Hasifi Shahrun requested bail of RM15,000 with one surety. Counsel Ammanjeet Singh, representing the accused, appealed for a lower bail amount, citing his client's young age, clean record, and limited financial means.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for KL, Putrajaya, Selangor and 9 states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to affect parts of 10 states until 5pm today. In the warning issued at 2.15pm, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather is forecast to affect the whole of Perlis, Penang, Perak, Kelantan and Selangor, as well as Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya. In Kedah, the affected districts are Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu.

items.title: Loke: Govt won’t meddle in port management appointments, Malaysians must retain 51pc ownership
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 17 — The government does not interfere in management appointments at companies operating ports, including those involving foreign nationals, as long as Malaysians retain majority ownership, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said. He said government policy requires companies holding concessions or strategic national assets such as ports to be at least 51 per cent Malaysian-owned. “As far as the government is concerned, we regulate only in terms of shareholding involving companies operating concessions and strategic national assets, including ports.

items.title: Bersatu info chief says PAS using ‘Machiavellian’ politics to push party out of Perikatan
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 17 — The bitter public dispute within Perikatan Nasional (PN) deepened today after Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia accused PAS of using “Machiavellian” tactics to sideline one of the coalition’s founding parties. In a Facebook post today, he claimed PAS had sought to push Bersatu out of PN through political manoeuvring during negotiations and preparations for the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections. “PAS’ actions in conducting behind-the-scenes negotiations, cutting ties with Bersatu ahead of the Johor state election, delaying PN’s preparations for the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state polls for certain purposes, and manoeuvring to remove Bersatu from PN are not the Islamic way, but PAS’ way.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz performs Friday prayers at Masjid Kariah Pantai
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Yang Dipertuan Besar Negri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir today joined more than 500 Muslims in performing Friday prayers at Masjid Kariah Pantai, here. His Royal Highness was accompanied by his two sons, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin and Tunku Zain Al-'Abidin. After the prayers, Tuanku Muhriz and his sons mingled with congregants and presented contributions to children who attended the Friday prayers.

items.title: Volkswagen workers to grill CEO on cuts at staff assemblies in August
items.summary: BERLIN: The works council of German carmaker Volkswagen said on Friday it will host extraordinary staff assemblies in August with CEO Oliver Blume, giving workers the opportunity to grill him on management plans to radically overhaul the company. Blume will attend meetings with staff at the company headquarters in Wolfsburg on August 25, and the plants in Emden and Zwickau on August 26, the works council said in an update to staff seen by Reuters. The restructuring could double current job cuts to 100,000, Blume has said.

items.title: Penang will not delay new water tariff, Chow says RM20m revenue crucial
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH, July 17 — Penang will not delay the implementation of the new water tariff that came into effect on July 1 any further, despite calls for a postponement, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said. He said the additional revenue from the new tariff, estimated at around RM20 million annually, is crucial to help the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) finance various projects to strengthen the state’s water supply. Meanwhile, PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the new water rates must be implemented to raise funds for urgent water supply projects to ensure water supply sufficiency in Penang, and further postponement would delay funding for the Water Contingency Plan 2030 (WCP 2030).

items.title: Akmal: Government remains vigilant despite resilient economic growth
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: Malaysia's estimated gross domestic product (GDP) growth of 5.8 per cent in the second quarter of 2026 reflects the resilience of the national economy and the effectiveness of Putrajaya's intervention measures. However, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government remained vigilant despite the encouraging projections, particularly amid ongoing geopolitical volatility in the Middle East. "Growth has reached 5.8 per cent, on top of the 5.4 per cent recorded in the first quarter.

items.title: Man allegedly attacked with sharp weapon while waiting for bus near university in Wangsa Maju
items.summary: According to Utusan Malaysia , the footage appears to show a man dressed in black suddenly attacking a man dressed in white multiple times with what appears to be a sharp weapon before kicking him. Police launched an investigation after the victim lodged a report. Wangsa Maju district police chief Assistant Commissioner Annas Sulaiman said an investigation paper had been opened under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means.

items.title: Food rider pleads not guilty to reckless riding charge over fatal Jalan Dewan Bahasa crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — A food delivery rider pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of reckless riding that allegedly caused the death of another motorcyclist yesterday. Muhammad Ridzuan Armis, 21, was charged with riding a Yamaha motorcycle recklessly, resulting in the death of Muhammad Nazri Shahadan, 47, on Jalan Dewan Bahasa, while travelling from Jalan Hang Tuah towards Jalan Istana, at about 2.30am on July 16. Prosecuting Officer Inspector Shazwan Hasifi Shahrun requested bail of RM15,000 with one surety.

items.title: Rahmah sales record 21mil transactions, RM238mil spent to ease living costs
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The government spent RM238.64 million on the Madani Rahmah Sales Programme nationwide between Jan 1 and July 13 this year, recording more than 21 million transactions. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Deputy Minister Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said more than 17,000 sales were held during the period, with the government targeting 30,000 programmes by the year-end. "The programme continues to receive an encouraging response as it helps ease the cost of living by offering essential goods at lower prices," she said after attending an event here today.

items.title: China set to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged for 14th month in July
items.summary: SHANGHAI: China is set to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged for a 14th consecutive month in July, a Reuters survey showed, despite softer-than-expected second-quarter economic data highlighting uneven growth in the world's second-largest economy. The loan prime rate (LPR), normally charged to banks' most creditworthy clients, is calculated each month after 20 designated commercial banks submit proposed rates to the People's Bank of China (PBOC). The strong market expectation for steady LPR fixings comes amid a persistent K-shaped divergence in the broader economy, where robust export growth continues to lead the recovery while domestic activity remains sluggish.

items.title: Penang proceeds with water tariff hike after year-long delay, says Chow
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: The Penang government will not further defer the implementation of the water tariff hike of 20 sen to 62 sen per cubic metre following a nearly year-long postponement. He said the federal government, through the National Water Services Commission (Span), had set the new water tariff on July 30 last year, and several other states had already enforced the rates. Chow said implementing the new tariff was crucial to ensure the state water operator had the financial capability to fund various projects to strengthen water supply security in Penang.

items.title: Azalina: Govt takes corporate mafia claims seriously, probes must proceed independently and transparently
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — The government takes seriously any allegations involving ‘corporate mafia’ activities that could undermine the integrity of, and public confidence in, public institutions, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. She said the ongoing investigations should be allowed to proceed independently, transparently and comprehensively, in accordance with the law and established procedures. She said the establishment of an RCI in Malaysia is governed by the Commissions of Enquiry Act 1950 (Act 119).

items.title: Seven & i shares jump 3pct on Zabka stake talks in Europe push
items.summary: TOKYO: Shares in Seven & i rose three per cent in Tokyo on Friday as the Japanese retailer said it was in talks to buy a stake in Polish convenience store operator Zabka Group as it seeks new growth drivers following investor pressure over weak earnings. Zabka has a market value of about US$8 billion. Aeon, Japan's largest retailer, also climbed three per cent while memory chipmaker Kioxia fell 16 per cent.

items.title: Witness tells Muhyiddin trial only hidden shareholder can explain RM1m payment to Bersatu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Bumiputera contractor Nepturis Sdn Bhd’s managing director Mohd Rizman Akum Khan today told the High Court that he was unsure if the company’s RM1 million cheque to political party Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia in February 2022 was a bribe, saying that only the company’s hidden shareholder Lian Tan Chuan (“TC Lian”) could answer. Rizman: I’m not sure, only TC Lian can answer. Rizman: Agree.

items.title: Fuziah: Govt spends RM239m on Rahmah sales from Jan to July 2026, logs 21 million transactions
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 17 — The government spent RM238.64 million on the implementation of the Madani Rahmah Sales Programme (PJRM) nationwide between January 1 and July 13 this year, during which more than 21 million transactions were recorded. Deputy Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Datuk Fuziah Salleh said more than 17,000 PJRMs had been held nationwide during the period, with the government targeting 30,000 programmes by the end of this year. She said that, to date, 606 retail outlets nationwide had been appointed as PJRM strategic partners, including supermarkets, mini markets, Agrobazaars and other retail premises.

items.title: DBKL anti-begging sweep nets 25 across Kuala Lumpur tourist belt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) said it took enforcement action against 25 people during a joint anti-begging operation across several city centre hotspots, with five later handed over to other government agencies for further action. DBKL said those subjected to enforcement action comprised 13 Malaysians and 12 foreign nationals. DBKL said it would continue strengthening strategic cooperation with enforcement and government agencies to curb begging activities in the capital.

items.title: Six charged with possessing more than six tonnes of subsidised cooking oil without licence
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 17 — Six men, including a Malaysian, were charged in the Sessions Court here today with possessing a total of 6,443 kilogrammes of subsidised cooking oil without a valid licence last Tuesday. Lorry driver Moo Teck Lim, 50, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read to him in Mandarin before Judge Fatimah Zahari. They were charged under Regulation 3(1) of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974, an offence punishable under Section 22(1) of the Control of Supplies Act 1961, read together with Section 21 of the same Act and Section 34 of the Penal Code.

items.title: Police warn against spreading false or provocative social media posts during Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 17 — Police have reminded the public to use social media responsibly throughout the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, including by refraining from spreading defamatory, seditious, insulting, provocative or false content. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) viewed seriously any dissemination of content that could undermine public order, threaten national security or disrupt social harmony. Alzafny said police would intensify monitoring of social media content throughout the election period, and any breaches of the law would be investigated regardless of the background or status of those involved.

items.title: PM Anwar: Biggest barrier to reform is resistance to change
items.summary: NILAI, July 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the biggest challenge in implementing national reforms stems not from a lack of technology or expertise, but from the reluctance of certain quarters to embrace change, including among members of the elite who continue to defend a culture of corruption and systemic weaknesses. Anwar said the government’s efforts to strengthen governance and combat corruption might not be popular, but they must continue to ensure the country’s administrative system becomes more transparent, accountable and effective. Anwar said that, according to the theory of change, society is generally divided into two groups: those with a propensity to change and those who exhibit resistance to change.

items.title: Two men remanded over murder of 10-year-old found dead in Sepanggar
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 17 — Two men — a foreign national and a local — were remanded for seven days, from today, to assist in the investigation into the murder of a 10-year-old girl whose body was found in Kampung Numbak, Sepanggar, yesterday. The remand order for the two suspects, aged 39 and 42, was issued by Magistrate Wan Farrah Farriza Wan Ghazali until July 24. Earlier, the two suspects were arrested following the discovery of the body of Nurjilya Abdullah, who had been reported missing since July 13.

items.title: Social media influencer pleads not guilty to rape of 14-year-old girl in Kangar
items.summary: KANGAR, July 17 — A social media influencer pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to raping a girl aged 14 years and 10 months, at a house in Jalan Tiong, Taman Kangar on June 17. Muhammad Iman Hazriq Azizan, 23, made the plea after the charge against him was read out before Judge Sharifah Norazlita Syed Salim Idid. The court fixed August 14 for mention of the case, with Deputy Public Prosecutor Nabilah Ahmad Poad appearing for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.

items.title: Indonesian woman sentenced to five years for unlawful abortion in Sarawak
items.summary: KUCHING, July 17 — The Sessions Court here has sentenced an Indonesian woman to five years’ imprisonment for intentionally taking misoprostol to medically abort her unborn son. Judge Musli Ab Hamid convicted Nopika, 21, on her guilty plea under Section 315 of the Penal Code for an act done with intent to prevent a child being born alive or to cause it to die after birth. According to the facts of the case, a medical officer attended to Nopika on the night of the incident after she complained of abdominal pain.

items.title: Anwar says AI-ready teachers must not lose sight of ethics
items.summary: NILAI, July 17 — Malaysia’s education system must produce teachers who not only master emerging fields in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM), such as artificial intelligence (AI), but are also grounded in ethics and human values, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Also present were Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and IPGKPT director Kartini Abdul Mutalib. What must be upheld are human values.

items.title: School reputations cannot come before bullied children, says Anwar
items.summary: NILAI, July 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded schools not to conceal bullying incidents to protect their reputation, stressing that swift action is essential to safeguard victims and address the problem effectively. Anwar said this at the Temu Anwar programme with students and staff of the Institute of Teacher Education (IPG) Technical Education Campus in Bandar Enstek today. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek was also present.

items.title: Langkawi police probe childcare centre after former teacher alleges children locked in toilet
items.summary: LANGKAWI, July 17 — Police are investigating a report lodged by a former teacher at a childcare centre in the district alleging the improper treatment of children at the premises. Langkawi police chief ACP Khairul Azhar Nuruddin said police had received the report on Wednesday from a woman who had been dismissed from the centre. Khairul Azhar said police were continuing their investigation to establish the validity of the allegations before taking any further action.

items.title: MOHE data show 96.6pc of public university undergraduates are Malaysians, foreigners mostly postgraduates
items.summary: According to the Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE), data from the MyMoheS system as of December 31, 2025, showed that international students accounted for 8.9 per cent of total enrolment at public universities, comprising 56,565 of the 634,706 students. MOHE said the majority of international students were enrolled in postgraduate programmes, while undergraduate programmes at public universities continued to give priority to local students. Overall, enrolment at public and private higher education institutions stood at 1,264,541 students, with international students making up 12.6 per cent, or 159,138 students.

items.title: KL’s Jalan Raja to close from July 21 to 27 ahead of Citrawarna 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Jalan Raja will close for nearly six days from next week as Kuala Lumpur prepares for Citrawarna 2026, with additional traffic restrictions planned during the festival’s cultural parade, Tourism Malaysia said today. Jalan Raja in front of Dataran Merdeka will be fully closed from noon on July 21 until 5am on July 27 to facilitate preparations, event operations and dismantling works for the annual festival. The closures will affect the route between Bulatan Dato’ Onn and Jalan Parlimen, extending to Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Esfahan, Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman and Jalan Tun Perak, as well as the Jalan Kinabalu access road to Jalan Sultan Hishamuddin.
